description:
## Background

`bsm-kia.14` extracts the inline sidebar selectors and per-spec
sidebar helpers into one canonical module. Without this expand
step every spec migration would have to invent its own helper,
perpetuating the shotgun-surgery pattern.

## What to build

`e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` exporting the canonical
sidebar assertion helpers used by all four WebDriver specs. The
helper owns the sidebar selector strings; specs import named
assertions only.

## Technical Approach

- Create `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts`.
- Implement and export:
  - `expectCurrentWorkspace(basename | path)` — assert the sidebar
    shows the expected Current Workspace, by basename or full
    path.
  - `expectNoCurrentWorkspace()` — assert the chooser is empty.
  - `expectRememberedWorkspace(basename)` — assert a remembered
    workspace appears in the chooser list.
- The selector string `"[aria-label='Workspace'] ..."` lives only
  inside this module.
- Do not import the helper from any spec yet — that is the
  migrate sub-issues' job.

## Considerations

- This module can sit next to `helpers/rpc.ts` (from
  `bsm-kia.13.1`) in the same `helpers/` directory.
- The selector inside this module must keep working as the React
  markup evolves. If `WorkspaceSelector.tsx` is renamed or
  restructured, this helper is the single point of fix.
- The helpers must produce clear failure messages (which selector
  fired, what was expected, what was found) — these messages are
  the only diagnostic a CI run will show.
- This task does not add new assertions beyond what exists today.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` exports the canonical
      sidebar helpers listed above.
- [ ] The literal selector string `[aria-label='Workspace']` (or
      any equivalent) appears only inside `helpers/sidebar.ts`,
      nowhere else.
- [ ] No spec imports the new module yet (this is the expand step).
- [ ] `pnpm exec ultracite check`, `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`, and
      `pnpm test` all pass.
- [ ] Existing `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and
      `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` still pass with no
      behavioral change.

## Test Plan

- Add a Vitest unit test file
  (`e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.test.ts`) that verifies:
  - Any pure formatter / matcher logic returns the expected
    value.
  - Selector construction (if parameterized) produces the
    expected strings for representative inputs.
- Run `pnpm test`, `pnpm exec ultracite check`, and
  `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` to prove no regression.
title: Expand: create e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts
